1→---
     2→# SeaweedFS Installation and Configuration Playbook
     3→# 
     4→# This playbook installs SeaweedFS cluster components:
     5→# - Master server for cluster coordination
     6→# - Volume servers for data storage
     7→# - Filer server for file system interface
     8→# - Collection-based organization for different artifact types
     9→#
    10→- name: Install and Configure SeaweedFS
    11→  hosts: linux_hosts
    12→  become: true
    13→  vars_files:
    14→    - ../vars/main.yml
    15→
    16→  tasks:
    17→    - name: Create SeaweedFS user
    18→      user:
    19→        name: seaweedfs
    20→        system: true
    21→        shell: /bin/false
    22→        home: /var/lib/seaweedfs
    23→        create_home: true
    24→
    25→    - name: Create SeaweedFS directories
    26→      file:
    27→        path: "{{ item }}"
    28→        state: directory
    29→        owner: seaweedfs
    30→        group: seaweedfs
    31→        mode: '0755'
    32→      loop:
    33→        - /var/lib/seaweedfs
    34→        - /var/lib/seaweedfs/master
    35→        - /var/lib/seaweedfs/volume1
    36→        - /var/lib/seaweedfs/volume2
    37→        - /var/lib/seaweedfs/filer
    38→        - /etc/seaweedfs
    39→        - /var/log/seaweedfs
    40→
    41→    - name: Check if SeaweedFS is already installed
    42→      stat:
    43→        path: /usr/local/bin/weed
    44→      register: weed_binary
    45→
    46→    - name: Get current SeaweedFS version (if installed)
    47→      command: /usr/local/bin/weed version
    48→      register: current_weed_version
    49→      changed_when: false
    50→      ignore_errors: true
    51→      when: weed_binary.stat.exists
    52→
    53→    - name: Download SeaweedFS
    54→      get_url:
    55→        url: "https://github.com/seaweedfs/seaweedfs/releases/download/{{ seaweedfs.version }}/linux_amd64.tar.gz"
    56→        dest: /tmp/seaweedfs.tar.gz
    57→        mode: '0644'
    58→      when: not weed_binary.stat.exists or seaweedfs.version not in (current_weed_version.stdout | default(''))
    59→
    60→    - name: Extract SeaweedFS
    61→      unarchive:
    62→        src: /tmp/seaweedfs.tar.gz
    63→        dest: /usr/local/bin
    64→        remote_src: true
    65→        owner: root
    66→        group: root
    67→        mode: '0755'
    68→      when: not weed_binary.stat.exists or seaweedfs.version not in (current_weed_version.stdout | default(''))
    69→
    70→    - name: Verify SeaweedFS installation
    71→      command: /usr/local/bin/weed version

    73→      changed_when: false
    74→
    75→    - name: Display SeaweedFS version

    78→
    79→    - name: Create SeaweedFS master service

    82→        dest: /etc/systemd/system/seaweedfs-master.service

    87→    - name: Create SeaweedFS volume service

    99→        owner: root
   100→        group: root
   101→        mode: '0644'
   102→
   103→    # SeaweedFS will run with command-line arguments, no config files needed
   104→
   105→    - name: Check SeaweedFS master service status
   106→      command: systemctl is-active seaweedfs-master
   107→      register: seaweedfs_master_status
   108→      ignore_errors: true

   110→
   111→    - name: Start and enable SeaweedFS master (if not running)

   150→

   158→
   159→    - name: Wait for filer to be ready
   160→      wait_for:

   164→
   165→    - name: Create artifact collections

   168→        method: PUT
   169→        status_code: [200, 201, 409]  # 409 = already exists
   170→      loop:
   171→        - name: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.artifacts.name }}"
   172→          replication: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.artifacts.replication }}"
   173→        - name: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.metadata.name }}"
   174→          replication: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.metadata.replication }}"
   175→        - name: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.debug.name }}"
   176→          replication: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.debug.replication }}"
   177→      register: collection_creation

   179→
   180→    - name: Display collection creation results

   183→      loop: "{{ collection_creation.results }}"
   184→
   185→    - name: Assign volumes to collections for single-node setup
   186→      uri:
   187→        url: "http://localhost:{{ seaweedfs.master_port }}/vol/grow?collection={{ item.collection }}&count={{ item.count }}&replication={{ seaweedfs_single_node.replication }}"
   188→        method: POST
   189→        status_code: [200, 400]  # 400 might indicate volumes already assigned
   190→      loop:
   191→        - collection: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.artifacts.name }}"
   192→          count: "{{ seaweedfs_single_node.volumes_per_collection.artifacts }}"
   193→        - collection: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.metadata.name }}"
   194→          count: "{{ seaweedfs_single_node.volumes_per_collection.metadata }}"
   195→        - collection: "{{ seaweedfs.collections.debug.name }}"
   196→          count: "{{ seaweedfs_single_node.volumes_per_collection.debug }}"
   197→      register: volume_assignment
   198→      failed_when: false
   199→
   200→    - name: Display volume assignment results
   201→      debug:
   202→        msg: "Volume assignment for {{ item.item.collection }}: {{ item.json.count if item.json is defined and 'count' in item.json else 'Failed or already assigned' }}"
   203→      loop: "{{ volume_assignment.results }}"
   204→
   205→    - name: Create ploy storage configuration for SeaweedFS

   212→      # Always create config since ploy user exists by design
   213→
   214→    - name: Set up firewall for SeaweedFS
   215→      ufw:

   223→    - name: Display SeaweedFS cluster status

   255→
   256→        - name: Create Consul service definition for SeaweedFS volume

   275→
   276→        - name: Create Consul service definition for SeaweedFS filer

     2→Description=SeaweedFS Master Server
     3→Documentation=https://github.com/seaweedfs/seaweedfs
     4→After=network.target
     5→
     6→[Service]
     7→Type=simple

    24→PrivateTmp=true
    25→ProtectSystem=strict
    26→ProtectHome=true
    27→ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/seaweedfs/master
    28→ReadOnlyPaths=/etc/seaweedfs
    29→
    30→[Install]
    31→WantedBy=multi-user.target

input.plan:
## Plan: Restructure SeaweedFS Deployment

### Overview
Reconfigure SeaweedFS deployment to run master and volume servers with systemd (for stable base infrastructure) and filer with Nomad (for dynamic scaling and better integration with API services).

### Changes Required:

#### 1. **Create Nomad Job Template for SeaweedFS Filer** (`nomad-seaweedfs-filer.hcl.j2`)
- Job type: `service` with high priority (75)
- Use `raw_exec` driver to run weed binary
- Download weed binary via artifact stanza
- Configure service discovery via Consul with DNS name `seaweedfs-filer.service.consul`
- Health checks on port 8888
- Environment variables for master connection

#### 2. **Update SeaweedFS Playbook** (`seaweedfs.yml`)
- Keep systemd services for master and volume servers
- Remove systemd service creation for filer
- Add task to deploy filer via Nomad job
- Ensure proper startup order: master → volume → filer (via Nomad)

#### 3. **Create Filer Deployment Playbook** (`seaweedfs-filer.yml`)
- Separate playbook for filer deployment/updates
- Generate Nomad job from template
- Deploy using nomad-job-manager.sh
- Verify service registration in Consul

#### 4. **Update Main Playbook** (`main.yml`)
- Ensure SeaweedFS master/volume are started before any Nomad deployments
- Add filer deployment step after Nomad is ready

#### 5. **Modify Service Templates**
- Keep `seaweedfs-master.service.j2` unchanged
- Keep `seaweedfs-volume.service.j2` unchanged
- Remove `seaweedfs-filer.service.j2` (replaced by Nomad job)

### Implementation Steps:

1. Create `nomad-seaweedfs-filer.hcl.j2` with proper Consul service registration
2. Update `seaweedfs.yml` to remove filer systemd service
3. Create `seaweedfs-filer.yml` playbook for Nomad deployment
4. Update orchestration in `main.yml` and `site.yml`
5. Test deployment ensuring filer is accessible via `seaweedfs-filer.service.consul:8888`
6. Verify API can connect to filer using Consul DNS

### Benefits:
- Master/volume servers remain stable with systemd
- Filer can be dynamically managed via Nomad
- Better integration with service mesh
- Consul DNS provides reliable service discovery
- Easier updates and rollbacks for filer component
